title: Can Dragons Truly Die? #got #shorts
description: Dragons seem unstoppable—but history tells a different story. From war to betrayal, dragons have fallen before. This is how dragons can die. #ASOIAF #Dragons #Shorts

scenes.narration: Dragons seem unstoppable—but history proves they are not immortal.
scenes.image_prompt: massive dragon wounded mid-flight with flames flickering unevenly, dark fantasy cinematic, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: In the books, dragons have been killed—through war, strategy, and sometimes betrayal.
scenes.image_prompt: battle scene with dragon under attack from multiple directions, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Despite their size and power, dragons are still physical creatures—and they can be wounded.
scenes.image_prompt: dragon with visible scars and injuries, grounded realism, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Their eyes, wings, and underbelly are vulnerable points, especially during flight or combat.
scenes.image_prompt: highlighted weak points on dragon anatomy during battle, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: In past wars, coordinated attacks and specialized weapons have brought dragons down.
scenes.image_prompt: siege weapons targeting dragon in sky, coordinated assault, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Even other dragons can kill them—making dragon battles some of the most destructive events in history.
scenes.image_prompt: two dragons clashing violently mid-air, fire and fury, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: But their greatest weakness may not be physical—it may be their bond with their rider.
scenes.image_prompt: dragon reacting emotionally as rider is in danger, connection emphasized, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: If that bond is broken, the dragon can become unpredictable, vulnerable, or even turn against itself.
scenes.image_prompt: dragon in chaos after losing rider, destructive uncontrolled behavior, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Over time, dragons also became smaller and weaker, suggesting their power is tied to the world itself.
scenes.image_prompt: small dragon contrasted with ancient massive dragon, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Because dragons are not immortal—they are powerful, but still part of a world where everything can fall.
scenes.image_prompt: fallen dragon silhouette beneath fading firelight, somber cinematic ending, vertical 1080x1920
